artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a university-wide Generative AI Use Policy PDF for PNU faculty, researchers, students, and administrative staff. Program and strategy pages were documented but rejected for claim evidence because they are not the binding GenAI use policy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff

artifacts.claimText: PNU's Generative AI Use Policy applies across PNU degree programs and to PNU faculty, researchers, students, administrative staff, and teaching, learning, research, training, development, and administrative activities.
artifacts.normalizedValue: university-wide generative AI use policy scope

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This policy applies to: All degree programs at PNU, at all levels and modes of study including full-time and part-time, campus-based and distance learning; Taught and research-based modules and programs, which include graduate studies; PNU faculty, researchers, students, and administrative staff; All teaching, learning, research, training, development, and administrative activities

artifacts.claimText: PNU faculty must disclose whether generative AI may be used in a course, include course-level guidelines on permissible and non-permissible uses, and include assignment disclaimers about appropriate and inappropriate generative AI use.
artifacts.normalizedValue: faculty course transparency and assignment disclaimer requirements

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: PNU faculty must indicate and be transparent about whether Generative AI can be used in the course and if so, provide clear guidance to students on how Generative AI may or may not be used. PNU faculty must include a section on 'Guidelines on Generative AI Use' in their course specification which outlines the permissible uses of generative AI for students which support student learning, and the non-permissible uses of generative AI in the course.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Policy Terms - For PNU Faculty, item 4, page 7
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: PNU faculty must avoid using AI-generated-content detection tools to check student work and instead use authentic assessment and trusting relationships with students.
artifacts.normalizedValue: faculty prohibited from using AI-detection tools for student work checks

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: PNU faculty must avoid using tools designed to detect AI Generative content to check student work, since there is no evidence to date which demonstrates that any Generative AI-detection tool reliably detects content generated by artificial intelligence, and tests have revealed significant margins of error.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Policy Terms - For PNU Faculty, item 8, page 8

artifacts.claimText: PNU students must uphold academic integrity when using generative AI, follow instructor guidance, acknowledge allowed AI use, and not claim AI-generated work as their own.
artifacts.normalizedValue: student academic integrity and disclosure requirements for GenAI use

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: PNU students must uphold academic integrity standards and ensure the originality and authenticity of their work, by ultimately owning their writing and critical thinking. In assignments where Generative AI tools are allowed, their use must be appropriately acknowledged and cited.

artifacts.claimText: PNU researchers must not use generative AI to misrepresent research, fabricate data, plagiarize, present AI-generated content as human-created content, or use generative AI in sensitive activities such as peer reviews or evaluations.
artifacts.normalizedValue: research integrity restrictions on GenAI use

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Use Policy - For PNU Faculty, Researchers, Students and Administrative Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: PNU researchers must not use Generative AI to misrepresent their research, such as fabricating data, plagiarizing the work of others, presenting AI-generated content as human-created content. PNU researchers must refrain from using Generative AI tools in sensitive activities such as peer reviews or evaluations.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Policy Terms - For PNU Researchers, items 8 and 15, pages 11-12

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged candidate because the policy scope text directly names the covered programs, people, and activities.
Approved as a teaching claim tied to explicit faculty policy terms about course specifications and assignment disclaimers.
Approved as a staged academic-integrity candidate because the source explicitly says faculty must avoid AI-detection tools for student work.
Approved as a staged student academic-integrity candidate; it does not infer beyond the student policy terms.
Approved as a staged privacy candidate using direct student policy text about personal and confidential data.
Approved as a staged research claim tied to explicit researcher policy terms on misrepresentation and sensitive activities.
Approved as source-status metadata about oversight and violation handling; it does not publish a canonical enforcement conclusion.

artifacts.title: Princess Nourah bint Abdulrahman University Generative AI Use Policy staged findings
artifacts.summary: Staged findings from PNU's official Generative AI Use Policy identify university-wide coverage, faculty course-guidance obligations, AI-detection-tool restrictions, student academic-integrity duties, privacy and confidentiality requirements, researcher integrity restrictions, and AI Center oversight.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged bundle uses one official English-language PDF policy; rejected discovery sources were not used for claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
